stories.title: Tripoli ARG, 31st MEU return from U.S. 5th, 7th Fleet deployment
stories.summary: The USS Tripoli and its accompanying amphibious ready group have completed a six-month deployment to the U.S. 5th and 7th Fleet areas of operations, offloading in Okinawa on July 22, 2026. The deployment spanned the Indo-Pacific and the Middle East, with the group supporting the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports and carrying out precision strikes. The 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit, part of the deployment, is now back in Okinawa. The forward-deployed naval expeditionary forces are built to pivot to wherever the nation needs them most.

stories.title: Trump greets the fallen as he faces a decision point on Iran
stories.summary: President Donald Trump attended the solemn transfer of four American service members' bodies at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware, marking a somber moment as he faces a decision point on escalating the Iran conflict. The bodies were returned in flag-draped cases, with Trump saluting and the transfer taking just under 20 minutes. This event comes as thousands of American troops are arrayed across the Middle East, risking retaliation and further danger. Trump's visit to Dover highlights the weight of responsibility for a president in times of war.

stories.title: Indian police cracked down on 'cockroach' protesters. They went home and made memes about it
stories.summary: Indian police cracked down on tens of thousands of protesters in Delhi, firing tear gas and charging demonstrators with batons on Monday. The protesters, supporters of the Cockroach Janta Party, demanded the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over exam paper leaks and recruitment scandals. The crackdown marked a violent turn in the largest anti-government demonstrations against Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government in the Indian capital in recent years. The protesters have since taken to social media to mock the police response with memes.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported a negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion for the first time in at least a decade, due to increasing spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company's AI spending is expected to reach $205 billion this year, a 7.9% increase from last year. This surge in costs comes as major tech companies invest heavily in AI technology. Alphabet's stock fell 4% in after-hours trading following the release of its financial results.

stories.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
stories.summary: Scottish athletes Marshall and Adlington have been chosen to carry the Scotland flag at the Commonwealth Games. The 59-year-old Marshall and 39-year-old Adlington will lead the procession into the opening ceremony. Marshall, a bowls player, and Adlington, a judo athlete, have been sworn to secrecy for weeks, making the announcement a surprise to many. They will officially take on their new roles at the Games in the coming days.

stories.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
stories.summary: A 4DX screening of Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" in Los Angeles resulted in 12 people being treated for seasickness. The three-hour film's intense visuals and motion effects caused the issue. Theaters are now offering wristbands to help viewers cope with the experience. Those who have already seen the film in 70mm and Imax formats are now trying out the 4DX version.

stories.title: Joshua not ready for death of friends to 'sink in'
stories.summary: Heavyweight boxer Anthony Joshua returns to the ring on Saturday for his first fight since a fatal car crash killed two close friends and team members, Sina Ghami and Latif Ayodele, on December 29, 2025. Joshua, who sustained minor injuries, will face Kristian Prenga in his first contest since knocking out Jake Paul. Joshua's friends were a sport and exercise rehabilitation coach and a personal trainer, respectively. He will attempt to focus on his fight despite the emotional weight of their loss.

stories.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
stories.summary: The US House of Representatives has passed a $1.15 trillion defense policy bill in a near party-line vote of 216-212, with most Democrats opposing the measure due to concerns over President Trump's ability to wage war in Iran without congressional authorization. The bill now heads to the Senate, where Democrats have already blocked a similar measure. Senate Republicans lack the votes to overcome Democratic opposition. The bill's fate remains uncertain as the two parties clash over the annual defense policy bill.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz continue. The potential targets include structures in or near the capital Tehran. This warning comes after 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran, with Tehran warning against targeting its nuclear facilities. The situation remains volatile with Iran's air defences activated in Tehran and reports of explosions in several cities.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing 23-year-old Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The Reds aim to beat Arsenal and Bayern Munich to the signature. Barcola's World Cup performance has boosted Liverpool's chances of securing the young forward. A deal could be finalized in the coming weeks.

stories.title: ‘Doctor Strange’ Director Says Elon Musk ‘Doesn’t Know S— About Cinema’ After Musk Vows to Make AI ‘Odyssey’: ‘His Opinions on Art Are Worthless’
stories.summary: Elon Musk has vowed to create an AI-generated film adaptation of Homer's "The Odyssey" by the end of the year, using his tool Grok. The project has drawn criticism from film director Scott Derrickson, who called Musk's opinions on art "worthless" due to his lack of understanding of cinema. Musk has been a vocal critic of director Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" since its release, citing "woke" casting decisions. The outcome of Musk's AI project remains to be seen, but it is expected to be a major test of the technology's capabilities.

stories.title: ‘Star Trek: Strange New Worlds’ Cast, Co-Showrunner Talk Playing With Genres in Season 4, ‘Joyful Curiosity,’ and That Puppet Episode
stories.summary: The cast and co-showrunner of "Star Trek: Strange New Worlds" discussed the upcoming Season 4, which features episodes blending multiple genres, including sci-fi thriller and musical elements. The series will continue to experiment with storytelling, offering a unique take on the "Star Trek" franchise. Anson Mount praised the experience of working with co-showrunners who asked the cast to suggest genres they wanted to act in. Season 4 premieres on Paramount+.

stories.title: A year after deadly jet crash at Bangladesh school, families demand answers
stories.summary: A year after a Bangladeshi air force jet crashed into a school in Dhaka, killing 36 people, mostly children, families and survivors are still seeking answers. The deadly incident on July 30, 2022, injured over 160 people, including students who are now struggling to return to normal life. Families of some victims gathered at a hospital in Dhaka on Tuesday to mark the anniversary, demanding justice and accountability. The investigation into the crash is ongoing.

stories.title: Meta won’t have to face the next planned social media addiction trial
stories.summary: A 15-year-old plaintiff from Florida has dropped a lawsuit against Meta, just days before a planned trial in a Los Angeles courtroom. The case, which was set to be the second in a series of bellwether trials, accused Meta of creating features that harmed and hooked teens. Meta will avoid a potential settlement, following similar settlements by TikTok, Snap, and YouTube. The plaintiff's lawyers cited a successful overall result of the litigation and a desire to focus on recovery and therapy.

stories.title: Blocked by censors, China's animal lovers take fight against abuse offline and overseas
stories.summary: Thousands of Chinese animal lovers are outraged over the killing of a stray dog and her puppies, sparking a wave of online protests that have been met with censorship. Authorities have scrubbed social media posts and urged people to withhold comments, citing the age of the four boys involved. The restrictions have only fueled the outcry, with animal lovers taking their fight offline and abroad, including putting up billboards in New York. The incident has drawn thousands of comments across social media in China three weeks after the event.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US has signed a landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, allowing the country to enrich uranium on its soil for the first time. The deal, worth billions of dollars, will be implemented at a US-built enrichment facility and is expected to advance US and regional security. The agreement upholds high standards of nuclear safety, security, and nonproliferation. The deal is set to strengthen the US competitive edge in civil nuclear technology.

stories.title: Trump’s Plan for Science: More Money for A.I., Less for Universities
stories.summary: Michael Kratsios, President Trump’s science adviser, proposed overhauling how the government funds research. Democrats said Mr. Trump’s actions had weakened science.

stories.title: Why Are Young People in India Protesting?
stories.summary: A youth-led movement angry over the education system and a lack of jobs is posing a rare challenge to a government that has ruled for more than a decade with a firm hand.

stories.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to $112 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
stories.summary: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA is set to unveil its first new wind tunnel in over 40 years at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The Flight Dynamics Research Facility will be showcased in a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and center director Dr. Trina Dyal will be available for media questions. The event is open to US citizens and lawful permanent residents, with RSVPs due by July 29.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

stories.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200%
stories.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower. The report recommends targeting ultra-processed foods, which are high in sugar and refined carbohydrates, as the primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. This shift in focus away from fat and salt is expected to lead to more effective public health policies. Manufacturers will now be held accountable for reformulating products to reduce sugar content.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
